metadata.title: States PCS: Karnataka GK for KPSC and other Karnataka State Exams

questions.question: Maski, the famous minor rock edict of Emperor Ashoka, bearing his name as Ashoka, is located in which district of Karnataka?
questions.explanation: Maski is a town and an archaeological site in the Raichur district of Karnataka. It lies on the bank of the Maski river which is a tributary of the Tungabhadra. Maski derives its name from Mahasangha or Masangi . The site came into prominence with the discovery of a minor rock edict of Emperor Ashoka by C. Beadon in 1915. It was the first edict of Emperor Ashoka that contained the name Ashoka in it instead of the earlier edicts that referred him as Devanampiye piyadasi. This edict was important to conclude that many edicts found earlier in the Indian sub-continent in the name of Devanampiye piyadasi, all belonged to Emperor Ashoka.

questions.question: Hire Benakal, which is in UNESCO World Heritage Site tentative list since 2021 is famous for which of the following?
questions.explanation: Hire Benakal is a megalithic site in Karnataka in Koppal district. It contains roughly 400 megalithic funerary monuments, dated to the transition period between Neolithic and the Iron Age periods. Known locally in the Kannada language as elu guddagalu, their specific name is Moryar Gudda. Hire Benakal is reported to be the largest necropolis among the 2000 odd megalithic sites found in South India, mostly in Karnataka. Since 1955, it has been under the management of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) within the Dharwad circle. Hire Benakal has been recognized as an UNESCO World Heritage Site tentative list on 19th may 2021.

questions.question: Where is the Nagareshwara temple located?
questions.explanation: Nagareshwara temple is located in Bankapura. Bankapura is a panchayat town in Haveri district of Karnataka. Bankapura is famous for the Nagareshwara temple, Bankapura fort, The Bankapura Peacock Sanctuary. Baada, the birthplace of Kanakadasa is near to Bankapura.

questions.question: Which river originates in Bangrabalige valley of Karnataka?
questions.explanation: The Netravati River or Netravathi Nadi originates from the Bangrabalige valley, Yelaneeru Ghat located in Kudremukh, Chikkamagaluru district of Karnataka. This river is considered as one of the Holy Rivers of India and it flows through the famous pilgrimage place Dharmasthala. Netravathi River merges with the Kumaradhara River at Uppinangadi before flowing to the Arabian Sea. This river is the main water source to Mangalore and Bantwal.

questions.question: Hanumangundi Falls is located in which district of Karnataka?
questions.explanation: Hanumanagundi Falls which is also known as the Suthanabbe Falls or the Soothanabbi Falls is located in the Kudremukh National Park in the Chikkamagaluru district of the state of Karnataka. This falls is located between the Lakya and Karkala Dam in the Kudremukh national park.

questions.question: Which Karnataka city is among India’s 7 cement clusters?
questions.explanation: Kalaburagi, also known as Gulbarga, is one of India’s seven cement clusters. It was transferred from Hyderabad State to Karnataka in 1956 during state reorganization. Karnataka is a top cement-producing state with about 17,253 million tons of limestone deposits. The state produces over 11 million tons of cement annually, contributing 6.51% of India’s total production. Karnataka has around 22 cement units with a combined capacity of 16 million metric tons per annum (MMTPA).

questions.question: Which District-Cluster pair is correct under Karnataka’s MSE-CDP?
questions.explanation: The District Industrial Cluster Development Program (MSE-CDP) in Karnataka supports various clusters such as textiles in Ballari district. Tumakuru is known for sports and fitness goods clusters, Bidar for agricultural implements, and Shivamogga for health and wellness clusters. These mappings are specified under Karnataka’s MSME initiatives to promote district-wise industrial growth through cluster development. Ballari has been identified for textile cluster promotion as per Karnataka industrial development schemes.

questions.question: Where is the headquarters of Ather Energy Pvt. Ltd. located?
questions.explanation: Ather Energy Pvt. Ltd. is headquartered in Bengaluru. It is an Indian electric vehicle company founded in 2013 by Tarun Mehta and Swapnil Jain. The company produces electric scooters like Ather 450X and Ather 450 Plus. Bengaluru is a major tech and startup hub in India, hosting several EV companies including Ather Energy.

questions.question: Mukka, is the suburb of which city of Karnataka?
questions.explanation: Mukka is a suburb of Mangalore city, Karnataka and is the gateway to Mangalore city from the north. It is also an educational centre in the state. Once upon a time it was a small town-village but from the year 2010 the region is developing both commercially and educationally. Though official language is Kannada but Tulu is the main language that is spoken here.

questions.question: What is Karnataka’s proposed revenue expenditure for 2026-27?
questions.explanation: Karnataka’s proposed revenue expenditure for 2026-27 is Rs 3,38,007 crore, showing a 12% increase over the revised estimate for 2025-26.
